channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Pakistan Urgently Seeks LNG
item.description: Pakistan is seeking to buy liquefied natural gas for delivery this week as a string of attacks in the Strait of Hormuz disrupts flows of the super-chilled fuel.

item.title: North America Rig Count Rises for 8 Straight Weeks
item.description: North America added 21 rigs week on week, according to Baker Hughes' latest North America rotary rig count.

item.title: Hormuz Continues to See Traffic despite Fresh Concerns
item.description: Ships continued to cross the Strait of Hormuz, though the number broadcasting their passage dropped, as some companies said vessel attacks over the weekend had made them less confident about navigation.

item.title: EBRD Pledges More Funding for Ukraine's Long-Term Energy Needs
item.description: The European Bank of Reconstruction and Development committed more financing for Ukraine to install new power generation capacity and help the country decarbonize its energy system.

item.title: Eldorado Drilling Seals Vantage Acquisition
item.description: 'The combination brings together Eldorado's Atlantic Zonda with Vantage's asset base, strong operating capabilities, global footprint, and proven track record managing high specification drilling assets'.

item.title: Lion Energy gains Indonesia’s approval for East Seram PSC farmout
item.description: Lion Energy has received formal approval from the Indonesian MEMR for the transfer of a 15% participating interest in the East Seram PSC from its subsidiary Balam Energy to OPIC East Seram.

item.title: Is Libya Quietly Becoming the Biggest Oil Prize the West Can’t Afford to Ignore?
item.description: At around the same time as OPEC raised its long-term oil demand forecast for the third consecutive year — now expecting global consumption to rise 19 million barrels per day (bpd), or 18%, by 2050 — Libya’s state-owned…

item.title: Oil Price Bubble Has Burst
item.description: Wood Mackenzie said the oil price bubble has burst.

item.title: Philippines Becomes World's Top Solar Panel Buyer
item.description: Philippines to the highest in the world since the Iran war began, according to customs data cited by Reuters. Amid soaring electricity prices and a supply crunch in fossil fuels, many Filipinos have opted to install…

item.title: Russia's Fuel Crisis Deepens as Putin Acknowledges Growing Gasoline Shortages
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin acknowledged what outside experts, many regional officials, and everyday citizens have already noticed: Russia is facing energy shortages, causing major problems for citizens and…

item.title: EU Secures Industry Deal to Grow Energy Storage Capacity
item.description: The European Commission executed a public-private cooperation agreement on energy storage that saw 22 member states commit to enabling the installation of 30-35 gigawatts of capacity over the next 2 years.

item.title: Pakistan Plans to Boost LPG Imports and Mulls Cheaper Oil Supply from Iran
item.description: Pakistan will raise its imports of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) from Iran and considers the idea of importing cheaper crude oil from the Islamic Republic, Pakistani Minister of Petroleum, Ali Pervaiz Malik, has said…

item.title: Halliburton, Valero and 3 More Stocks Set Up for a Fragile Hormuz Truce
item.description: Brent crude has fallen more than 20% in the past month, sliding from triple digits during the worst of the Iran war to around $72 a barrel today. WTI sits near $70. That kind of move usually means one thing: the crisis…

item.title: Oil trade stabilises amid US-Iran halt to renewed hostilities
item.description: Oil prices stabilised on 29 June, Monday after the US and Iran reached an agreement to halt their renewed hostilities in the Gulf and committed to resuming negotiations over the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: OEUK Says Urgent Decisions Needed on Rosebank, Jackdaw
item.description: Industry body Offshore Energies UK said 'urgent decisions' are needed on the Rosebank and Jackdaw projects 'for national security'.

item.title: Gulf Producers Race to Load Oil and LNG as Hormuz Stays Open
item.description: Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, and Qatar continue loading oil and LNG onto vessels at their Persian Gulf ports despite the attacks on two commercial vessels near the Strait of Hormuz and the weekend tensions…

item.title: Zephyr Energy acquires 27,000 acres in Utah’s Paradox Basin
item.description: Zephyr Energy has expanded its operated land position in the Paradox Basin, Utah, US, with the acquisition of approximately 27,000 acres of additional leases.

item.title: Trump Signals Diplomatic Opening After Days of U.S.-Iran Strikes
item.description: After some persistent Sunday reports, including in The Wall Street Journal, said that last week's renewed tit-for-tat fighting between the US and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz had 'stalled' the next round of talks…

item.title: More Well Workers offshore Norway Will Join Strike
item.description: The Norwegian Union of Energy Workers said it had sent a notice that an additional 63 members would join hundreds already on strike after government mediation failed to yield a new Well Service Agreement.

item.title: Pakistan LNG Seeks Emergency Cargo After Weekend Hormuz Flare-Up
item.description: Pakistan’s state LNG importer is urgently seeking to procure an LNG cargo for delivery this week, with offers due on June 29, as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains volatile amid persistent threats to tankers…
